title: Malware Detection Rate Trending
spl:
index=security tag=malware
| timechart span=1d count by signature
description: Tracks EDR/AV detection volume normalized as `Malware` CIM for executive and SOC trending.
value: Tracks EDR/AV detection volume normalized as `Malware` CIM for executive and SOC trending.
implementation: Whitelist known PUPs; alert on new `signature` or 3× baseline for same family.
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.signature, _time span=1d
knownFalsePositives: Malware and quarantine events from re-scans after cleanups, slow endpoints finishing remediation, and test infections in sandboxes; confirm host state with EDR.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Defender, CrowdStrike, etc. → Malware.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `Malware_Attacks` objects.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Whitelist known PUPs; alert on new `signature` or 3× baseline for same family.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=malware
| timechart span=1d count by signature
```

Understanding this SPL

**Malware Detection Rate Trending** — Tracks EDR/AV detection volume normalized as `Malware` CIM for executive and SOC trending.

Documented **Data sources**: `Malware_Attacks` objects. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Defender, CrowdStrike, etc. → Malware. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by signature** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.signature, _time span=1d
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Malware Detection Rate Trending** — Tracks EDR/AV detection volume normalized as `Malware` CIM for executive and SOC trending.

Documented **Data sources**: `Malware_Attacks` objects. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Defender, CrowdStrike, etc. → Malware. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Malware.Malware_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked area (families), Line chart (total).
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Malware Detection Rate Trending" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Malware.Malware_Attacks` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
